name: UnauthorizedAccessIntrusion
description: A case template for handling suspected or confirmed unauthorized access or intrusion incidents, including validation, evidence preservation, scoping, containment, root-cause analysis, impact assessment, remediation, and post-incident follow-up.
author: Zachary Carter
summary: Investigation of a suspected or confirmed unauthorized access or intrusion incident involving compromise of systems, accounts, applications, services, or infrastructure. Scope, access path, attacker activity, affected assets, and business impact are under investigation.

tasks.title: Validate and classify intrusion event
tasks.description: Determine whether the observed activity represents confirmed unauthorized access, attempted intrusion, suspicious but unconfirmed activity, misconfiguration, legitimate administrative activity, or a false positive. Identify the initial systems, accounts, or services involved.

tasks.title: Initiate stakeholder coordination and escalation
tasks.description: Notify and coordinate with the appropriate internal stakeholders, including system owners, infrastructure teams, identity teams, security leadership, management, legal, privacy, and other affected parties as needed based on scope and severity.

tasks.title: Identify affected systems, accounts, and services
tasks.description: Determine the affected hosts, user accounts, service accounts, applications, remote access paths, administrative interfaces, storage locations, and business services. Document confirmed and suspected scope.

tasks.title: Preserve evidence and relevant records
tasks.description: Preserve relevant system logs, authentication logs, EDR telemetry, network telemetry, volatile evidence, host artifacts, cloud logs, remote access records, process execution history, registry or configuration artifacts, file system evidence, and other records needed to support a defensible investigation.

tasks.title: Determine initial access path
tasks.description: Investigate how the intrusion began, such as compromised credentials, exposed remote access, brute force, phishing, exploited vulnerability, web application compromise, malware delivery, insider misuse, or third-party access. Document the suspected or confirmed entry point.

tasks.title: Investigate persistence, privilege escalation, and lateral movement
tasks.description: Determine whether the threat actor established persistence, escalated privileges, moved laterally, accessed administrative tools, abused scheduled tasks or services, created accounts, modified policies, or used remote execution techniques to expand access.

tasks.title: Assess affected data, systems, and business impact
tasks.description: Determine whether data was accessed, copied, modified, deleted, or exfiltrated, and assess impact to system integrity, service availability, customer-facing operations, regulated data, and organizational risk.

tasks.title: Contain affected systems, identities, and access paths
tasks.description: Restrict unauthorized access by isolating hosts, disabling or restricting compromised accounts, revoking sessions, rotating credentials and keys, blocking malicious infrastructure, disabling exposed services, restricting administrative access, and implementing emergency controls while preserving critical evidence where possible.

tasks.title: Eradicate malicious artifacts and unauthorized access
tasks.description: Remove malware, backdoors, persistence mechanisms, malicious accounts, unauthorized tools, malicious tasks or services, and other attacker footholds. Eliminate the original access path and confirm that unauthorized access has been removed.

tasks.title: Remediate vulnerabilities, credentials, and control gaps
tasks.description: Patch exploited systems, correct insecure configurations, reset or rotate credentials, revoke tokens, replace exposed secrets, harden remote access paths, review trust relationships, and address control weaknesses identified during the investigation.

tasks.title: Increase monitoring and validate recovery
tasks.description: Increase monitoring on affected systems, identities, applications, and network paths. Validate that containment and eradication actions were effective, confirm there is no ongoing attacker activity, and monitor for reintrusion or delayed persistence.

tasks.title: Assess legal, privacy, compliance, and reporting obligations
tasks.description: Determine whether legal, privacy, contractual, regulatory, or policy obligations apply based on the type of intrusion, affected systems, and impacted data. Document notification and reporting decisions and required coordination.

tasks.title: Implement security improvements and close identified gaps
tasks.description: Improve detection coverage, logging, access control, segmentation, MFA, remote access security, vulnerability management, incident response procedures, and other defenses based on lessons learned from the intrusion.

tasks.title: Document findings and close case
tasks.description: Document the intrusion path, affected assets, attacker activity, persistence, impact, containment actions, remediation steps, reporting decisions, and lessons learned. Confirm required follow-up actions are complete before case closure.

notes.title: Initial alert or report
notes.content: Document how the suspected intrusion was identified, when it was discovered, and why the case was opened.

notes.title: Current status
notes.content: Track the current phase of the investigation, key updates, blockers, and next steps.

notes.title: Decision log
notes.content: Record major decisions, approvals, escalations, and significant events during the investigation.

notes.title: Stakeholders
notes.content: List involved stakeholders, including technical teams, management, legal, privacy, compliance, and business owners.

notes.title: Affected systems and services
notes.content: Document affected hosts, servers, endpoints, applications, remote access systems, administrative services, and infrastructure components.

notes.title: Affected accounts and identities
notes.content: Track user accounts, service accounts, admin accounts, tokens, certificates, or other identities relevant to the intrusion.

notes.title: Affected business functions
notes.content: Document impacted business services, departments, customers, partners, or operational processes.

notes.title: External infrastructure
notes.content: Record attacker IPs, domains, URLs, C2 infrastructure, VPN sources, cloud resources, or other external infrastructure tied to the case.

notes.title: Timeline of events
notes.content: Build a defensible timeline of relevant system, account, network, process, and administrative activity.

notes.title: Initial access findings
notes.content: Document the suspected or confirmed initial access path and supporting evidence.

notes.title: Persistence and privilege escalation
notes.content: Summarize persistence mechanisms, account changes, privilege escalation activity, and other footholds established by the threat actor.

notes.title: Lateral movement and execution
notes.content: Track lateral movement, remote execution, tool transfer, service creation, scripting activity, and related attacker actions.

notes.title: Data access and exfiltration findings
notes.content: Document evidence of data access, staging, compression, transfer, deletion, modification, or exfiltration.

notes.title: Preserved evidence
notes.content: List preserved artifacts, logs, triage data, memory captures, forensic images, exports, and other evidence retained for the case.

notes.title: Collection notes
notes.content: Document how evidence was collected, by whom, and any collection limitations or constraints.

notes.title: Chain of custody
notes.content: Track evidence handling, storage, transfer, and access history where required.

notes.title: Gaps and missing data
notes.content: Document missing logs, overwritten artifacts, visibility gaps, or other evidentiary limitations affecting the investigation.

notes.title: Containment actions
notes.content: Document host isolation, account restrictions, session revocations, infrastructure blocks, emergency control changes, and other containment measures.

notes.title: Credential and access changes
notes.content: Track password resets, key or secret rotation, token revocation, MFA changes, and trust or access-control modifications.

notes.title: Eradication actions
notes.content: Summarize malware removal, persistence removal, unauthorized account cleanup, patching, configuration fixes, and related remediation actions.

notes.title: Validation of recovery
notes.content: Document how recovery and eradication were validated and how ongoing attacker activity was ruled out or monitored.

notes.title: Business impact
notes.content: Document operational, financial, customer-facing, reputational, and mission-related impacts associated with the intrusion.

notes.title: Legal and privacy coordination
notes.content: Track discussions, guidance, and decisions involving legal, privacy, compliance, and reporting stakeholders.

notes.title: Notification decisions
notes.content: Record notification obligations, rationale, recipients, and timelines for any internal or external reporting.

notes.title: Executive updates
notes.content: Document status updates, decision points, and escalations communicated to leadership.

notes.title: Findings summary
notes.content: Provide a concise summary of the intrusion, affected assets, attacker activity, root cause, and investigative conclusions.

notes.title: Control improvements
notes.content: Document recommended improvements to monitoring, access control, segmentation, hardening, remote access, logging, and response procedures.

notes.title: Lessons learned
notes.content: Capture lessons learned, process gaps, detection gaps, and follow-up recommendations.
